data.text: I’m reading the bead contract and the read-side store API first, then I’ll map every callsite before changing signatures. After that I’ll add the requested deterministic tests, run the bead package tests, run pre-commit, and commit once everything is green.

data.text: I’ve confirmed the read-side APIs are still variadic in the core store and the archive lookup hardcodes `context.Background()`. Next I’m checking every implementation and the small set of non-test callsites so I can migrate them in one pass without touching the mutation APIs.

data.text: The search confirmed the main code path I need to fix is smaller than the raw grep suggested: the store methods themselves, the archive resolver, and a handful of internal callers like inject/bench/tests. I’m now enumerating the exact callsites with simpler repository-wide searches so I can change only the affected read APIs and leave mutations alone.

I’m making the contract change now: typed ctx-first read methods in the bead package plus the matching reader interfaces in the agent package. After that I’ll run the package tests to expose the remaining callsites and fix them systematically.

The signature changes are in. I’m compiling the bead package first to surface the immediate breakages before widening to the rest of the repo, because that will keep the fix-up loop tighter and avoid chasing stale errors from half-updated callsites.

data.text: I’ve got a large batch of one-arg `Get` and empty `ReadAll` calls in the agent package. Rather than patching those file by file, I’m applying a narrowly scoped codemod that only touches the store-like receiver names we actually identified, so it won’t rewrite unrelated `registry.Get(...)` calls.

input.command: /bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '390,440p' cmd/bead.go && printf '\\n====\\n' && sed -n '1280,1310p' cmd/bead.go && printf '\\n====\\n' && sed -n '2035,2060p' cmd/bead.go"

data.output: sed: can't read internal/server/graphql/integration_test.go: No such file or directory

data.text: I’ve handled the obvious command and server read-call sites. The remaining compile failures should now be concentrated in the agent package and a couple of GraphQL/server tests, so I’m updating those next before rerunning the repo compile pass.

data.text: The repo compile pass exposed two categories now: a bad codemod edge case in one agent test, and a handful of files that need an explicit `context` import after the new callsites were inserted. I’m fixing the syntax regression first, then I’ll add the missing imports and patch the remaining non-`store` receivers like `primaryStore` and `spokeStore`.

data.text: The bad codemod only broke the receiver text, not the underlying argument values. I’m checking a few representative files to see whether the original receiver names are obvious from local scope; if they are, I can repair them with a targeted replacement instead of reverting a lot of clean work.

I’ve confirmed the breakage is mechanical: the argument text survived, and the receiver name was dropped. Most of these agent tests still use a local `store` variable, so I’m checking the few `inner`/`beadStore` cases before I choose the repair rule, because those need the original receiver name preserved rather than a blanket `store` substitution.

I’m classifying the affected agent tests by their local store variable names now. That lets me repair the bad receiver substitutions in batches by receiver type instead of hand-editing hundreds of callsites.

	t.Run("stale_snapshot_selects_next_ready_bead", func(t *testing.T) {
		baseStore := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
		require.NoError(t, baseStore.Init())

		parent := &bead.Bead{
			ID:       "ddx-stale-parent",
			Title:    "Decomposed parent",
			Priority: 0,
			Labels:   []string{"decomposed"},
			Extra:    map[string]any{bead.ExtraExecutionElig: false},
		}
		next := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-stale-next", Title: "Next ready bead", Priority: 1}
		require.NoError(t, baseStore.Create(parent))
		require.NoError(t, baseStore.Create(next))

		staleParent := *parent
		staleParent.Extra = map[string]any{}
		staleStore := &staleReadyStore{
			Store:      baseStore,
			staleReady: []bead.Bead{staleParent, *next},
		}

		var executed []string
		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
			Store: staleStore,
			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
				executed = append(executed, beadID)
				return ExecuteBeadReport{
					BeadID:    beadID,
					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
					SessionID: "sess-stale-next",
					ResultRev: "feedbeef",
				}, nil
			}),
		}

		var sink bytes.Buffer
		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{EventSink: &sink})
		require.NoError(t, err)
		require.NotNil(t, result)
		assert.Equal(t, []string{next.ID}, executed, "worker must skip the stale parent and dispatch the next ready bead")
		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Attempts)
		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Successes)

		events := parseLoopEvents(t, sink.String())
		skips := loopEventDataByType(events, "picker.skip_stale_candidate")
		require.Len(t, skips, 1)
		assert.Equal(t, parent.ID, skips[0]["bead_id"])
		assert.Equal(t, "decomposed", skips[0]["reason"])
		assert.Equal(t, "pre_claim", skips[0]["stage"])
		assert.Equal(t, false, skips[0]["execution_eligible"])
		assert.Equal(t, true, skips[0]["decomposed"])

		claimed := loopEventDataByType(events, "bead.claimed")
		require.Len(t, claimed, 1)
		assert.Equal(t, next.ID, claimed[0]["bead_id"], "stale parent must not be claimed")

		got, err := store.Get(context.Background(), parent.ID)
		require.NoError(t, err)
		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, got.Status, "skipped parent must remain open")
		assert.Empty(t, got.Owner, "skipped parent must not remain claimed")
	})

	t.Run("stale_snapshot_idles_with_skip_event", func(t *testing.T) {
		baseStore := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
		require.NoError(t, baseStore.Init())

		parent := &bead.Bead{
			ID:       "ddx-stale-idle",
			Title:    "Only stale parent",
			Priority: 0,
			Labels:   []string{"decomposed"},
			Extra:    map[string]any{bead.ExtraExecutionElig: false},
		}
		require.NoError(t, baseStore.Create(parent))

		staleParent := *parent
		staleParent.Extra = map[string]any{}
		staleStore := &staleReadyStore{
			Store:      baseStore,
			staleReady: []bead.Bead{staleParent},
		}

		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
			Store: staleStore,
			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
				t.Fatalf("executor must not run for an execution-ineligible stale parent")
				return ExecuteBeadReport{}, nil
			}),
		}

		var sink bytes.Buffer
		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
			Once:      true,
			EventSink: &sink,
		})
		require.NoError(t, err)
		require.NotNil(t, result)
		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Attempts)
		assert.True(t, result.NoReadyWork, "worker must idle once the stale parent is skipped")

		events := parseLoopEvents(t, sink.String())
		skips := loopEventDataByType(events, "picker.skip_stale_candidate")
		require.Len(t, skips, 1)
		assert.Equal(t, parent.ID, skips[0]["bead_id"])
		assert.Equal(t, "decomposed", skips[0]["reason"])
		assert.Equal(t, "pre_claim", skips[0]["stage"])

		got, err := store.Get(context.Background(), parent.ID)
		require.NoError(t, err)
		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, got.Status)
		assert.Empty(t, got.Owner)
	})
}

package agent

	"encoding/json"

func TestPreClaimACQuality_AllMechanicalACs_Passes(t *testing.T) {
	acceptance := "1. `ParseAcceptance` function exists in accheck\n2. TestFooBar passes\n3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green\n"
	result := CheckACQuality(acceptance, DefaultACQualityMinScore)
	assert.True(t, result.PassesThreshold)
	assert.Equal(t, 3, result.Total)
	assert.Equal(t, 3, result.VerifiableCount)
	assert.Equal(t, 0, result.ProseCount)
	for _, item := range result.Items {
		assert.True(t, item.Verifiable, "AC #%d should be verifiable but kind=%s: %s", item.AC, item.Kind, item.Text)
	}
}

func TestPreClaimACQuality_AllProseACs_BlocksClaim(t *testing.T) {
	acceptance := "1. Improve logging clarity\n2. Make error messages cleaner\n3. Code is more readable\n"
	result := CheckACQuality(acceptance, DefaultACQualityMinScore)
	assert.False(t, result.PassesThreshold)
	assert.Equal(t, 3, result.Total)
	assert.Equal(t, 0, result.VerifiableCount)

The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Do not mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; do not use parallel tool calls for `git add`, `git commit`, or other staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 still allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add`, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.   </instructions>   <bead id="ddx-cc298105">     <title>bead store: migrate read APIs to ctx-first signatures</title>     <description> PROBLEM
TD-027 requires the foundational bead Store read surface to be typed and ctx-first, but the read methods still accept untyped variadic arguments. This keeps the local bead package API ambiguous and makes the ddx-464feba2 caller sweep too large to execute as one bead.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/backend.go:80-84 declares BeadReader with ReadAll(args ...any), ReadAllFiltered(args ...any), and Get(args ...any).
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:181-240 exposes Store.ReadAll(args ...any) and Store.ReadAllFiltered(args ...any), using storeCallContext from cli/internal/bead/store.go:134-141.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:599-625 exposes Store.Get(args ...any), using argument scanning for the bead ID.
- cli/internal/bead/archive.go:158-174 exposes Store.GetWithArchive(id string) and currently supplies context.Background() internally instead of accepting caller context.

PROPOSED FIX
Change only the read-side Store methods to typed ctx-first signatures: ReadAll(ctx context.Context), ReadAllFiltered(ctx context.Context, pred func(Bead) bool), Get(ctx context.Context, id string), and GetWithArchive(ctx context.Context, id string). Update BeadReader in cli/internal/bead/backend.go. Migrate only read-method callsites across the repo to pass context explicitly. Add deterministic bead package tests that prove canceled contexts are honored and GetWithArchive forwards the supplied context instead of creating a background context.

NON-SCOPE
- Init and GenID signature migration.
- Mutation APIs: Create, Update, and Close.
- Event method ctx threading: AppendEvent, Events, and EventsByKind.
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.
- Caller migration unrelated to ReadAll, ReadAllFiltered, Get, and GetWithArchive.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT
- TD-027.

PARENT/DEPS
- Parent/split source: ddx-464feba2.
- Deps: no additional deps; this child can land independently of the setup/id and mutation signature children.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. cli/internal/bead/store.go exposes ReadAll(ctx context.Context), ReadAllFiltered(ctx context.Context, pred func(Bead) bool), and Get(ctx context.Context, id string) with typed parameters and no variadic argument parsing for those methods.
2. cli/internal/bead/archive.go exposes GetWithArchive(ctx context.Context, id string) and passes ctx through to both active and archive lookups.
3. cli/internal/bead/backend.go exposes BeadReader methods with the same ctx-first typed read signatures.
4. All Go callsites for Store.ReadAll, Store.ReadAllFiltered, Store.Get, and Store.GetWithArchive pass an explicit context and typed arguments.
5. Add or update deterministic tests named TestStoreReadAll_RejectsCanceledContext, TestStoreReadAllFiltered_RejectsCanceledContext, TestStoreGet_RejectsCanceledContext, and TestStoreGetWithArchive_UsesSuppliedContext.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -run 'TestStore(ReadAll|ReadAllFiltered|Get|GetWithArchive)_' passes.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>phase:iterate, area:bead, area:storage, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-3fd19af1" base-rev="59e036221a7357130ea3e1ca08fed6e5ff8fd695" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260606T082428-e5ac4f62"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. - cli/internal/bead/store.go:627-655 exposes Store.Update(args ...any), scans for id and mutate function.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1499-1535 exposes Store.Close(args ...any), scans for id and then calls lifecycle helpers without explicit caller context.

PROPOSED FIX
Change only the mutation-side Store methods to typed ctx-first signatures: Create(ctx context.Context, b *Bead), Update(ctx context.Context, id string, mutate func(*Bead)), and Close(ctx context.Context, id string). Update BeadLifecycle in cli/internal/bead/backend.go. Migrate only Create, Update, and Close callsites across the repo to pass context explicitly. Add deterministic bead package tests for canceled-context behavior and typed argument validation.

NON-SCOPE
- Init and GenID signature migration.
- Read APIs: ReadAll, ReadAllFiltered, Get, and GetWithArchive.
- Event method ctx threading: AppendEvent, Events, and EventsByKind.
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.
- Caller migration unrelated to Create, Update, and Close.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT
- TD-027.

PARENT/DEPS
- Parent/split source: ddx-464feba2.
- Deps: no additional deps; this child can land independently of the setup/id and read signature children.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. cli/internal/bead/store.go exposes Create(ctx context.Context, b *Bead) error, Update(ctx context.Context, id string, mutate func(*Bead)) error, and Close(ctx context.Context, id string) error with typed parameters and no variadic argument parsing for those methods.
2. cli/internal/bead/backend.go exposes BeadLifecycle.Create(ctx context.Context, b *Bead) error while preserving Apply(ctx context.Context, id string, op Operation) error.
3. All Go callsites for Store.Create, Store.Update, and Store.Close pass an explicit context and typed arguments.
4. Add or update deterministic tests named TestStoreCreate_RejectsCanceledContext, TestStoreUpdate_RejectsCanceledContext, and TestStoreClose_RejectsCanceledContext.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -run 'TestStore(Create|Update|Close)_RejectsCanceledContext' passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>phase:iterate, area:bead, area:storage, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-3fd19af1" base-rev="c7cf172c21c5192d6322d3bacdeedbda5f811eef" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260606T083907-e599ac89"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. - cli/internal/bead/archive.go:158-174 exposes Store.GetWithArchive(id string) and currently supplies context.Background() internally instead of accepting caller context.

PROPOSED FIX
Change only the read-side Store methods to typed ctx-first signatures: ReadAll(ctx context.Context), ReadAllFiltered(ctx context.Context, pred func(Bead) bool), Get(ctx context.Context, id string), and GetWithArchive(ctx context.Context, id string). Update BeadReader in cli/internal/bead/backend.go. Migrate only read-method callsites across the repo to pass context explicitly. Add deterministic bead package tests that prove canceled contexts are honored and GetWithArchive forwards the supplied context instead of creating a background context.

NON-SCOPE
- Init and GenID signature migration.
- Mutation APIs: Create, Update, and Close.
- Event method ctx threading: AppendEvent, Events, and EventsByKind.
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.
- Caller migration unrelated to ReadAll, ReadAllFiltered, Get, and GetWithArchive.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT
